description: Explicitly displaying itemized prices and per-person costs in F&B content to build trust and help customers make informed purchasing decisions.
structural_recipe: Include specific prices for individual dishes (in SAR) and total per-person costs. Structure posts with: location tag → dish names → prices → optional quality assessment. Place invoice/receipt in carousel last slide or mention final bill amount. Use both Arabic and English pricing labels for clarity. Pair prices with high-quality food photography to justify costs.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers value transparency and cost-consciousness, especially during Ramadan and special occasions. Displaying prices upfront reduces purchase friction, builds credibility, and aligns with Islamic values of fair pricing. Clear pricing enables social comparison and word-of-mouth recommendation based on value proposition.
transplantation_caveats: Prices must be current and accurate to avoid reputation damage and customer complaint
High prices without corresponding presentation quality may trigger negative engagement
Currency must be clearly labeled (SAR) for local audience trust
Promotional pricing or seasonal rates should be explicitly marked to avoid misleading customers
cultural_constraints: Pricing should reflect Ramadan-appropriate value perception during Islamic month
Buffet and group dining options resonate more than individual high-priced items
Luxury pricing justified through presentation, heritage, or premium sourcing (truffles, wagyu, French cuisine)
